title: acmella radicans var. debilis
intro:
Acmella radicans var. debilis, also known as toothache plant, is a tropical plant native to Central and South America, known for its unique ability to numb the tongue and other areas of the mouth when its leaves are chewed.

The medicinal properties of Acmella radicans var. debilis include its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing toothaches, and relieving headaches and mouth sores.

The health benefits of Acmella radicans var. debilis include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. The plant's analgesic properties make it a popular natural remedy for pain management.

Acmella radicans var. debilis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used topically to relieve pain and inflammation, or taken orally to promote overall health and well-being.
